examples.title: Algorithm: IoT Analytics Pipeline
examples.description: Pseudocode demonstrating the complete IoT data analytics pipeline from data ingestion to actionable insights
examples.code:
ALGORITHM IoT_Analytics_Pipeline
INPUT: Raw sensor data stream from IoT devices
OUTPUT: Actionable insights and visualizations

CONSTANTS
 BUFFER_SIZE = 1000
 THRESHOLD_TEMP = 80
 THRESHOLD_PRESSURE = 200

DATA STRUCTURES
 SensorReading = {device_id, timestamp, sensor_type, value, location}
 AggregatedData = {time_window, device_count, avg_value, min_value, max_value}
 Alert = {severity, device_id, message, timestamp}

// ============ STAGE 1: DATA INGESTION ============
PROCEDURE Ingest_Data(device_id, data)
 BEGIN
 reading <- Parse_Sensor_Data(device_id, data)
 Validate_Data(reading)
 IF reading.is_valid THEN
 Publish_To_Stream(reading)
 ELSE
 Log_Data_Error(reading)
 ENDIF
 END

// ============ STAGE 2: DATA STORAGE ============
PROCEDURE Store_Raw_Data(reading)
 BEGIN
 timestamp <- reading.timestamp
 key <- Generate_TimeSeries_Key(reading.device_id, timestamp)
 Write_To_TimeSeries_DB(key, reading)
 END

// ============ STAGE 3: STREAM PROCESSING ============
PROCEDURE Process_Stream(reading)
 BEGIN
 // Real-time filtering and transformation
 IF reading.sensor_type = "temperature" THEN
 celsius <- Convert_Fahrenheit_To_Celsius(reading.value)
 reading.value <- celsius
 ENDIF
 
 // Update running statistics
 Update_Window_Statistics(reading)
 
 // Check for anomalies
 IF Detect_Anomaly(reading) THEN
 Trigger_Alert(reading)
 ENDIF
 
 RETURN reading
 END

// ============ STAGE 4: BATCH PROCESSING ============
PROCEDURE Run_Batch_Analytics(time_range)
 BEGIN
 data <- Retrieve_Historical_Data(time_range)
 
 // Descriptive Analytics
 summary <- Compute_Summary_Statistics(data)
 
 // Diagnostic Analytics
 correlations <- Find_Correlations(data)
 root_cause <- Identify_Anomaly_Root_Cause(correlations)
 
 // Predictive Analytics
 model <- Train_Prediction_Model(data)
 forecasts <- Generate_Forecasts(model, horizon)
 
 // Prescriptive Analytics
 recommendations <- Optimize_Operations(forecasts)
 
 RETURN {summary, root_cause, forecasts, recommendations}
 END

// ============ STAGE 5: VISUALIZATION ============
PROCEDURE Generate_Insights(results)
 BEGIN
 dashboard <- Create_Dashboard()
 Add_Chart(dashboard, "Historical Trends", results.summary)
 Add_Alerts_Panel(dashboard, results.anomalies)
 Add_Forecast_Panel(dashboard, results.forecasts)
 Add_Recommendations_Panel(dashboard, results.recommendations)
 
 Publish_Dashboard(dashboard)
 END

// ============ ANOMALY DETECTION ============
FUNCTION Detect_Anomaly(reading)
 BEGIN
 historical_avg <- Get_Moving_Average(reading.device_id, window=100)
 standard_dev <- Get_Standard_Deviation(reading.device_id)
 
 z_score <- (reading.value - historical_avg) / standard_dev
 
 IF ABS(z_score) > 3 THEN
 RETURN TRUE // Anomaly detected
 ELSE
 RETURN FALSE
 ENDIF
 END

// ============ MAIN EXECUTION ============
BEGIN
 WHILE system_active DO
 FOR EACH incoming_data IN data_stream DO
 Ingest_Data(incoming_data.device_id, incoming_data)
 ENDFOR
 
 // Periodic batch processing (every hour)
 IF current_time % 3600 = 0 THEN
 results <- Run_Batch_Analytics(past_24_hours)
 Generate_Insights(results)
 ENDIF
 ENDWHILE
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates the complete IoT analytics pipeline: (1) Data Ingestion - collecting raw sensor data from devices with validation, (2) Storage - writing to time-series databases, (3) Stream Processing - real-time filtering, transformation, and anomaly detection, (4) Batch Processing - descriptive, diagnostic, predictive, and prescriptive analytics on historical data, (5) Visualization - creating dashboards with insights. The anomaly detection uses z-score methodology to identify readings beyond 3 standard deviations.

examples.title: Python: IoT Data Processing and Analytics
examples.description: Python implementation demonstrating IoT data collection, streaming processing, and the four types of analytics
examples.code:
"""
IoT Data Analytics Implementation
Demonstrates: Data ingestion, stream processing, and all four types of analytics
"""
import json
import time
import random
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
from collections import deque
import statistics

# Simulated IoT Device Data Generator
class IoTSensorSimulator:
 """Simulates IoT devices generating sensor data"""
 
 def __init__(self, device_id, sensor_type, min_val, max_val):
 self.device_id = device_id
 self.sensor_type = sensor_type
 self.min_val = min_val
 self.max_val = max_val
 self.value = random.uniform(min_val, max_val)
 
 def read(self):
 """Generate next sensor reading with some variation"""
 noise = random.uniform(-0.1, 0.1) * (self.max_val - self.min_val)
 self.value = max(self.min_val, min(self.max_val, self.value + noise))
 
 return {
 'device_id': self.device_id,
 'sensor_type': self.sensor_type,
 'value': round(self.value, 2),
 'timestamp': datetime.now().isoformat(),
 'location': f"Zone_{random.randint(1, 5)}"
 }

# Data Buffer for Stream Processing
class DataBuffer:
 """Buffer for storing recent sensor readings"""
 
 def __init__(self, max_size=1000):
 self.buffer = deque(maxlen=max_size)
 
 def add(self, reading):
 self.buffer.append(reading)
 
 def get_all(self):
 return list(self.buffer)
 
 def get_by_device(self, device_id):
 return [r for r in self.buffer if r['device_id'] == device_id]

# Stream Processor for Real-time Analytics
class StreamProcessor:
 """Processes IoT data streams in real-time"""
 
 def __init__(self):
 self.buffer = DataBuffer()
 self.alerts = []
 self.thresholds = {
 'temperature': 80.0,
 'pressure': 200.0,
 'humidity': 90.0
 }
 
 def process_reading(self, reading):
 """Process single sensor reading"""
 self.buffer.add(reading)
 
 # Real-time anomaly detection
 sensor_type = reading['sensor_type']
 if sensor_type in self.thresholds:
 if reading['value'] > self.thresholds[sensor_type]:
 alert = {
 'type': 'THRESHOLD_EXCEEDED',
 'device_id': reading['device_id'],
 'sensor': sensor_type,
 'value': reading['value'],
 'threshold': self.thresholds[sensor_type],
 'timestamp': reading['timestamp']
 }
 self.alerts.append(alert)
 print(f" ⚠️ ALERT: {sensor_type} exceeded! {reading['value']} > {self.thresholds[sensor_type]}")
 
 return reading

# Analytics Engine implementing all four analytics types
class IoTAnalyticsEngine:
 """Implements Descriptive, Diagnostic, Predictive, and Prescriptive Analytics"""
 
 def __init__(self):
 self.historical_data = []
 
 # DESCRIPTIVE ANALYTICS: What happened?
 def descriptive_analytics(self, data):
 """Summarize historical data to understand what happened"""
 if not data:
 return {}
 
 values = [r['value'] for r in data]
 
 return {
 'total_readings': len(data),
 'average_value': round(statistics.mean(values), 2),
 'min_value': min(values),
 'max_value': max(values),
 'std_deviation': round(statistics.stdev(values), 2) if len(values) > 1 else 0,
 'devices_count': len(set(r['device_id'] for r in data)),
 'time_range': {
 'start': data[0]['timestamp'],
 'end': data[-1]['timestamp']
 }
 }
 
 # DIAGNOSTIC ANALYTICS: Why did it happen?
 def diagnostic_analytics(self, data):
 """Find root causes of anomalies or patterns"""
 if len(data) < 10:
 return {'message': 'Insufficient data for diagnostic analysis'}
 
 # Group by location
 location_groups = {}
 for r in data:
 loc = r['location']
 if loc not in location_groups:
 location_groups[loc] = []
 location_groups[loc].append(r['value'])
 
 # Find locations with highest average
 location_avgs = {
 loc: statistics.mean(vals) 
 for loc, vals in location_groups.items()
 }
 
 highest_location = max(location_avgs, key=location_avgs.get)
 
 return {
 'location_analysis': location_avgs,
 'anomaly_source': highest_location,
 'correlation_strength': 'Strong' if len(data) > 50 else 'Weak',
 'root_cause_hypothesis': f"Location {highest_location} shows elevated readings"
 }
 
 # PREDICTIVE ANALYTICS: What will happen?
 def predictive_analytics(self, data, forecast_hours=2):
 """Predict future values using moving average trend"""
 if len(data) < 5:
 return {'message': 'Insufficient data for prediction'}
 
 values = [r['value'] for r in data]
 
 # Simple linear trend prediction
 n = len(values)
 if n < 2:
 return {'predicted_value': values[-1]}
 
 # Calculate trend
 avg_change = (values[-1] - values[0]) / n
 last_value = values[-1]
 
 # Forecast
 predictions = []
 for i in range(1, forecast_hours + 1):
 predicted = last_value + (avg_change * i * 10) # Scale for hourly
 predictions.append({
 'hours_ahead': i,
 'predicted_value': round(predicted, 2),
 'confidence': max(50, 95 - (i * 10)) # Decreasing confidence
 })
 
 return {
 'current_trend': 'increasing' if avg_change > 0 else 'decreasing',
 'trend_magnitude': round(abs(avg_change), 4),
 'forecasts': predictions,
 'model_accuracy': f"{min(90, 50 + (n // 5))}%"
 }
 
 # PRESCRIPTIVE ANALYTICS: What should we do?
 def prescriptive_analytics(self, predictions, current_state):
 """Recommend optimal actions based on predictions"""
 recommendations = []
 
 # Check if any prediction exceeds threshold
 for pred in predictions:
 if pred['predicted_value'] > 75: # Near threshold
 recommendations.append({
 'priority': 'HIGH',
 'action': 'REDUCE_LOAD',
 'reason': f"Predicted value {pred['predicted_value']} approaching threshold",
 'expected_impact': 'Reduce readings by 15-20%',
 'automated': False
 })
 
 if not recommendations:
 recommendations.append({
 'priority': 'NORMAL',
 'action': 'MAINTAIN_STATUS_QUO',
 'reason': 'All predicted values within normal range',
 'expected_impact': 'Continue normal operations',
 'automated': True
 })
 
 return {
 'recommendations': recommendations,
 'optimization_strategy': 'minimize_energy_consumption',
 'confidence_score': predictions[0]['confidence'] if predictions else 0
 }

# Main Demonstration
def main():
 print("=" * 60)
 print("IoT DATA ANALYTICS DEMONSTRATION")
 print("=" * 60)
 
 # Initialize components
 sensors = [
 IoTSensorSimulator('SENSOR_001', 'temperature', 60, 85),
 IoTSensorSimulator('SENSOR_002', 'temperature', 55, 75),
 IoTSensorSimulator('SENSOR_003', 'pressure', 150, 210),
 ]
 
 processor = StreamProcessor()
 analytics = IoTAnalyticsEngine()
 
 # Simulate data collection
 print("\n📡 Collecting IoT sensor data...")
 for i in range(20):
 for sensor in sensors:
 reading = sensor.read()
 processor.process_reading(reading)
 time.sleep(0.1) # Simulate time between readings
 
 # Get all collected data
 all_data = processor.buffer.get_all()
 temp_data = [r for r in all_data if r['sensor_type'] == 'temperature']
 
 # DESCRIPTIVE ANALYTICS
 print("\n" + "=" * 60)
 print("1️⃣ DESCRIPTIVE ANALYTICS: What happened?")
 print("=" * 60)
 desc_results = analytics.descriptive_analytics(temp_data)
 print(f" Total Readings: {desc_results.get('total_readings', 0)}")
 print(f" Average Temperature: {desc_results.get('average_value', 0)}°F")
 print(f" Min: {desc_results.get('min_value', 0)}°F, Max: {desc_results.get('max_value', 0)}°F")
 print(f" Standard Deviation: {desc_results.get('std_deviation', 0)}")
 
 # DIAGNOSTIVE ANALYTICS
 print("\n" + "=" * 60)
 print("2️⃣ DIAGNOSTIVE ANALYTICS: Why did it happen?")
 print("=" * 60)
 diag_results = analytics.diagnostic_analytics(all_data)
 print(f" Location Analysis: {diag_results.get('location_analysis', {})}")
 print(f" Root Cause: {diag_results.get('root_cause_hypothesis', 'Unknown')}")
 
 # PREDICTIVE ANALYTICS
 print("\n" + "=" * 60)
 print("3️⃣ PREDICTIVE ANALYTICS: What will happen?")
 print("=" * 60)
 pred_results = analytics.predictive_analytics(temp_data)
 print(f" Current Trend: {pred_results.get('current_trend', 'N/A')}")
 print(f" Model Accuracy: {pred_results.get('model_accuracy', 'N/A')}")
 print(" Forecasts:")
 for forecast in pred_results.get('forecasts', [])[:2]:
 print(f" - {forecast['hours_ahead']}hr ahead: {forecast['predicted_value']}°F (conf: {forecast['confidence']}%)")
 
 # PRESCRIPTIVE ANALYTICS
 print("\n" + "=" * 60)
 print("4️⃣ PRESCRIPTIVE ANALYTICS: What should we do?")
 print("=" * 60)
 presc_results = analytics.prescriptive_analytics(
 pred_results.get('forecasts', []),
 desc_results.get('average_value', 0)
 )
 for rec in presc_results.get('recommendations', []):
 print(f" Priority: {rec['priority']}")
 print(f" Action: {rec['action']}")
 print(f" Reason: {rec['reason']}")
 print(f" Automated: {rec['automated']}")
 
 print("\n" + "=" * 60)
 print("✅ Analytics Complete!")
 print("=" * 60)

if __name__ == "__main__":
 main()
examples.explanation: This Python implementation demonstrates the complete IoT analytics workflow: (1) IoTSensorSimulator generates realistic sensor data with noise, (2) StreamProcessor handles real-time data ingestion with threshold-based alerting, (3) IoTAnalyticsEngine implements all four types of analytics - Descriptive (statistics summary), Diagnostic (location-based root cause analysis), Predictive (trend-based forecasting), and Prescriptive (action recommendations based on predictions). The code shows how raw IoT data flows through the analytics pipeline to produce actionable insights.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for streaming, O(n log n) for batch analytics

examples.title: C: IoT Sensor Data Collection System
examples.description: C implementation demonstrating IoT sensor data collection, buffering, and real-time processing for resource-constrained edge devices
examples.explanation: This C implementation demonstrates IoT data collection optimized for edge/embedded devices: (1) Circular buffer for efficient streaming data storage without dynamic allocation, (2) SensorReading struct for structured IoT data, (3) Device statistics tracking (min, max, sum, count), (4) Anomaly detection using threshold comparison, (5) Statistical calculations including moving average and standard deviation. This is suitable for resource-constrained IoT devices that need local data processing before sending to the cloud.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for buffer operations, O(n) for statistics calculations
examples.spaceComplexity: O(BUFFER_SIZE) for circular buffer, O(MAX_READINGS) per device for historical data
